agent_scopes.mechanism: Two scopes: project (.gemini/agents/*.md) and user/global (~/.gemini/agents/*.md). Both directories are scanned at session start and merged into a single registry; explicit collision-precedence behavior is not documented upstream.

definition_format.mechanism: Markdown files with YAML frontmatter (delimited by ---) in .gemini/agents/*.md (project) or ~/.gemini/agents/*.md (user/global); the file body becomes the subagent's system prompt.

invocation_patterns.mechanism: Two patterns: (1) automatic delegation by the main agent based on task relevance and the subagent's description, (2) explicit @-mention at the start of a prompt (e.g., '@codebase_investigator review this'); /agents list/enable/disable/configure manages the registry.

model_selection.mechanism: model frontmatter field (string); accepts a specific model id (e.g., 'gemini-3-preview') or 'inherit' (default) which uses the parent session's model. Optional 'temperature' field (0.0-2.0, default 1) sets per-subagent sampling temperature.

per_agent_mcp.mechanism: mcpServers object in frontmatter; declares inline MCP server configurations scoped to this subagent. Each entry follows the same shape as the global mcpServers config and is isolated to the subagent's invocation.

subagent_spawning.mechanism: Recursion is explicitly prevented — subagents cannot invoke other subagents even when granted the '*' tool wildcard. The documentation states this is to avoid infinite loops and excessive token usage.

tool_restrictions.mechanism: tools array in frontmatter; supports wildcards: '*' for all built-in and MCP tools, 'mcp_*' for all MCP tools, 'mcp_<server-name>_*' for tools from a single MCP server. If omitted, the subagent inherits the full tool set from the parent session.

providerExtensions.name: Local vs remote subagent kind
providerExtensions.summary: Optional 'kind' frontmatter field (string) accepts 'local' (default) or 'remote'; remote subagents are documented separately and support a list format that allows multiple remote subagents per file.

providerExtensions.name: Per-subagent turn limit
providerExtensions.summary: max_turns frontmatter field (number, default 30) caps the number of conversation turns a subagent may take before it must return to the main agent.

providerExtensions.name: Per-subagent execution timeout
providerExtensions.summary: timeout_mins frontmatter field (number, default 10) caps the wall-clock execution time of a subagent invocation.

providerExtensions.name: Per-subagent model temperature
providerExtensions.summary: temperature frontmatter field (number 0.0-2.0, default 1) sets the model sampling temperature for the subagent independently of the parent session.

providerExtensions.name: Tool wildcard patterns
providerExtensions.summary: Tool allowlists support wildcards: '*' (all tools), 'mcp_*' (all MCP tools), 'mcp_<server>_*' (per-server). Wildcards apply to the subagent's effective tool set at invocation time.

providerExtensions.name: Built-in expert subagents
providerExtensions.summary: Gemini CLI ships built-in subagents alongside user-authored ones: @codebase_investigator (codebase analysis), @cli_help (CLI docs), @generalist (multi-file operations), @browser_agent (experimental web automation).

providerExtensions.name: /agents management command
providerExtensions.summary: /agents list, enable, disable, configure, and reload manages the subagent registry; /agents reload rescans both ~/.gemini/agents and .gemini/agents without restarting the CLI.

providerExtensions.name: Remote subagents
providerExtensions.summary: Remote subagents (kind: remote) are also Markdown files with YAML frontmatter at the same locations; a list format permits multiple remote subagents in a single file (mixed local+remote or multiple local agents in one file is not supported).
